methodology.description: Five-gate criteria: (1) business quality, (2) valuation, (3) price dislocation, (4) moat durability, (5) decline reason. Insider buying is a bonus signal, not a gate.
methodology.full_signal_criteria: All 4 gates pass AND total ≥ 95 AND decline reason ≠ FUNDAMENTAL_CONCERN
methodology.hard_rejects: Revenue declining >5% YoY | Within 8% of 52wH | No market data
methodology.score_breakdown: Quality 0-40 | Valuation 0-30 | Dislocation 0-30 | Moat 0-40 | Insider bonus 0-15

full_signal_candidates.name: TransMedics

decline_reason.label: Sentiment disconnect
decline_reason.explanation: Revenue growing 37.1% while stock is 54.4% below its high — the fundamentals and price are moving in opposite directions. This is the setup.

signals: FCF $134M (22.1% FCF margin) — strong cash generation
Standalone moat — not in supply chain hierarchy
full_signal_candidates.moat_summary: Transplant center relationships + regulatory approvals + network effects.

full_signal_candidates.name: BWX Technologies

decline_reason.label: Sharp recent drop — investigate
decline_reason.explanation: Stock dropped 15.8% in one month, but fundamentals appear intact. A specific event likely triggered this — understand the catalyst before acting.

signals: Level-2 supply chain — underappreciated, 2-4 competitors, high switching costs
gaps: P/FCF 93.6× — expensive on cash
full_signal_candidates.moat_summary: Legal monopoly. Federal law requires US Navy nuclear propulsion components from BWXT — no substitute, no foreign alternative permitted. Zero AI disruption risk. Not a speculative SMR play — mandated defense infrastructure with revenue pre-sold.

Level-1 supply chain — core/obvious play, moat real but widely known
Moat: Government contract stickiness. Security clearance requireme
gaps: P/S 66.1× — very expensive, high conviction needed
